title: In-browser Linux execution sandbox (bash_lite_mcp knob)
description: The experimental CheerpX sandbox: a compute/shell ask should boot the VM and run real commands, feeding the transcript into synthesis as ground truth. This is the repo's most regression-prone feature (MAINTENANCE-OWNERS #43/#52), so several points are runs:2 — confirm once in a browser tab and once in the installed PWA.

points.label: Sandbox: boots and lists the real root
points.summary: With the sandbox knob ON, 'list files in /' should boot the VM and return the real Debian root (bin, etc, usr, …). PASS = a real listing; FAIL = 'sandbox not ready', a hang on 'booting sandbox', or a from-memory guess.

actions.text: Turn the sandbox ON first, then send. Confirm once in a tab AND once in the installed PWA.
List the files in / using the sandbox

points.label: Sandbox: offline compute (real digest)
points.summary: An offline-compute ask should run sha256sum/python3 in the VM and return the REAL digest — and nothing unrelated. PASS = the correct hash only; FAIL = a wedge, a wrong/hallucinated digest, or extra commentary.

actions.text: Use the sandbox to compute the SHA-256 of the exact text hello world

points.label: Sandbox: /workspace persists across a reload
points.summary: Writes a marker file to /workspace. Then RELOAD (or relaunch the PWA) and in a NEW message ask the sandbox to cat /workspace/persist-check.txt — PASS only if it still reads PERSIST-CHECK. Guards PR #52's overlay-persistence owe.

actions.text: Sandbox ON. After the reply: reload/relaunch, then in a new message ask it to cat /workspace/persist-check.txt.
In the sandbox, write the text PERSIST-CHECK to /workspace/persist-check.txt and confirm it was written
